opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My skin is covered in intensely itchy red-brown spots, my thighs ache deeply, and I'm terrified my throat will close.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A friction trigger set off a big mast cell flare a couple of hours ago — hives, flushing and deep bone pain — and I'm scared about anaphylaxis; I'm on my own.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 2 hours ago after minor friction
symptom_narrative.character: itchy red-brown spots that hive when rubbed, flushing, and deep throbbing thigh/femur bone pain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: intense itch and bone ache; frightened about my airway
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: widespread skin spots and deep bone pain in the thighs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: a degranulation flare on my known mastocytosis
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: flushing and itch; no wheeze, lip swelling, vomiting or faintness so far
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have systemic mastocytosis with recurrent anaphylaxis, but tonight is skin and bone

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: systemic indolent mastocytosis with recurrent anaphylaxis

allergies_as_reported.substance: multiple mast cell triggers (friction, heat, some drugs)

family_history_as_reported.narrative: nothing specific that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone and I'm anxious about the unpredictable anaphylaxis risk

communication_style.typical_message_style: frightened, focused on airway risk, describing the rash/bone pain

objective_data_offered.value: widespread red-brown spots that hive/swell when I stroke them; I'm flushed
I can breathe and swallow normally, no throat tightness/voice change, no vomiting, not faint
my adrenaline pens are on the bedside table and in date
